categories.name: User Experience Design
categories.description: Guidelines 2.1–2.21 — designing experiences that are efficient, inclusive, and respectful of user attention.

guidelines.title: Understand user requirements or constraints, resolving barriers to access
guidelines.detail: All tools work with standard PDF files in any modern browser. No account, plugin, or installation required.

guidelines.title: Minimize non-essential content, interactivity, or journeys
guidelines.detail: Each tool is a single-purpose page: drop a file, get a result. No onboarding wizards, pop-ups, or multi-step funnels.

guidelines.title: Ensure that navigation and wayfinding are well-structured
guidelines.detail: Flat navigation: home page lists all 36 tools. Each tool has a descriptive name, icon, and direct URL.

guidelines.title: Design to assist and not to distract
guidelines.detail: No autoplaying media, no animations beyond minimal transitions, no infinite scroll.

guidelines.title: Avoid being manipulative or deceptive
guidelines.detail: No dark patterns, no fake urgency, no forced sign-ups. Tool results are delivered immediately without gates.

guidelines.title: Use a design system for interface consistency
guidelines.detail: Tailwind CSS utility classes with a shared component library ensure visual consistency across all tools.

guidelines.title: Provide clear, inclusive content with purpose
guidelines.detail: Each tool has a clear title, description, and expected input/output format. Available in multiple languages via i18n.

guidelines.title: Optimize media to reduce resource use
guidelines.detail: Minimal imagery. Icons are inline SVGs. No stock photos, hero images, or video backgrounds.

guidelines.title: Ensure animation is proportionate and easy to control
guidelines.detail: Only CSS transitions for state changes (hover, toggle). Respects prefers-reduced-motion media query.

guidelines.title: Use optimized and appropriate web typography
guidelines.detail: System font stack — no custom web fonts downloaded. Zero font-related network requests.

guidelines.title: Offer suitable alternatives for every format used
guidelines.detail: Output is always a downloadable file. Multiple conversion formats available (PDF, images, text, EPUB).

guidelines.title: Provide accessible, usable, minimal web forms
guidelines.detail: Tool interfaces use minimal form elements: a file input and optional settings. No mandatory fields beyond the file itself.

guidelines.title: Avoid unwanted notifications
guidelines.detail: Zero push notifications. No email collection. No permission requests for notifications or location.

guidelines.title: Reduce the impact of downloadable and physical documents
guidelines.detail: Tools like compress-pdf, grayscale-pdf, and remove-blank-pages directly reduce document size and print impact.

guidelines.title: Audit and test for bugs or issues requiring resolution
guidelines.detail: SCI profiler runs automated benchmarks across all 36 tools. Build pipeline includes type checking and linting.

guidelines.title: Regularly test and maintain compatibility
guidelines.detail: Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari. Uses standard Web APIs and progressive enhancement.

categories.name: Web Development
categories.description: Guidelines 3.1–3.20 — writing efficient, secure, standards-based code.

guidelines.title: Set goals based on performance and energy impact
guidelines.detail: SCI profiler measures energy consumption and carbon emissions per tool. Results published in the SCI Report page.

guidelines.title: Remove unnecessary or redundant information
guidelines.detail: Production build uses Vite tree-shaking. Dev-only code (SCI profiler) is stripped from production bundles.

guidelines.title: Modularize bandwidth-heavy components
guidelines.detail: Each tool is a lazy-loaded Web Worker. Only the selected tool's code is loaded on demand.

guidelines.title: Remove unnecessary code
guidelines.detail: Vite tree-shaking + code splitting. Dead code elimination verified by grepping production bundles.

guidelines.title: Avoid redundancy and duplication in code
guidelines.detail: Shared utilities (runInWorker, file helpers) are centralized. Tool implementations follow a uniform pattern.

guidelines.title: Give third parties the same priority as first parties during assessment
guidelines.detail: Google AdSense is the only third-party script. It is loaded asynchronously and does not block rendering.

guidelines.title: Ensure code follows good semantic practices
guidelines.detail: Semantic HTML5 elements (main, nav, section, article). ARIA attributes where needed.

guidelines.title: Defer the loading of non-critical resources
guidelines.detail: Route-based code splitting with dynamic imports. Tool workers load only when the user selects a tool.

guidelines.title: Provide information to help understand the usefulness of a page
guidelines.detail: SEO meta tags on every page. Structured descriptions for each tool. Open Graph metadata for sharing.

guidelines.title: Structure metadata for machine readability
guidelines.detail: SEO composable generates title, description, and Open Graph tags for every route.

guidelines.title: Use sustainability beneficial user preference media queries
guidelines.detail: Supports prefers-color-scheme (dark mode) and prefers-reduced-motion. No unnecessary resource loading for either preference.

guidelines.title: Ensure layouts work for different devices and requirements
guidelines.detail: Fully responsive design with Tailwind breakpoints (sm, md, lg). Mobile-first approach.

guidelines.title: Use standards-based JavaScript and APIs
guidelines.detail: Standard Web APIs: File API, Web Workers, Blob, performance.now(). No proprietary browser APIs.

guidelines.title: Ensure that your code is secure
guidelines.detail: All processing is client-side. No data leaves the browser. No server-side attack surface for user files.

guidelines.title: Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance
guidelines.detail: Minimal dependency tree. Core libraries (pdf-lib, pdfjs-dist, tesseract.js) are actively maintained open-source projects.

guidelines.title: Include expected and beneficial files
guidelines.detail: Includes robots.txt, sitemap, privacy policy, and this sustainability page.

guidelines.title: Use the most efficient solution for your service
guidelines.detail: Client-side processing eliminates server round-trips entirely. Files never leave the user's device.

guidelines.title: Use the latest stable language version
guidelines.detail: TypeScript with ES2022 target. Vite build with modern browser output.

categories.name: Hosting, Infrastructure, and Systems
categories.description: Guidelines 4.1–4.12 — efficient hosting, caching, and data transfer.

guidelines.title: Use sustainable hosting
guidelines.detail: Hosted on GitHub Pages — a static CDN. No dedicated server running 24/7 for this application.

guidelines.title: Optimize caching and support offline access
guidelines.detail: Static assets served with long-lived cache headers via GitHub Pages CDN. Content-hashed filenames enable aggressive caching.

guidelines.title: Reduce data transfer with compression
guidelines.detail: GitHub Pages serves gzip/brotli compressed assets. Vite produces minified JS/CSS bundles.

guidelines.title: Setup necessary error pages and redirection links
guidelines.detail: Custom 404.html redirects to hash-based SPA routing. No broken links.

guidelines.title: Avoid maintaining unnecessary virtualized environments or containers
guidelines.detail: No server infrastructure at all. The entire application is a static site — zero containers, zero VMs.

guidelines.title: Consider the impact and requirements of data processing
guidelines.detail: All processing happens on the user's existing device. No additional server energy consumption per operation.

guidelines.title: Use Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) appropriately
guidelines.detail: GitHub Pages CDN distributes static assets globally. Minimal latency, no origin server required.

guidelines.title: Store data according to the needs of your users
guidelines.detail: No user data stored whatsoever — not on servers, not in databases, not in cookies (beyond consent).

guidelines.title: Have an ethical and sustainable product strategy
guidelines.detail: Privacy-first by design. No data collection, no tracking, no user profiling. Open source codebase.

guidelines.title: Communicate the environmental impact of user choices
guidelines.detail: SCI Report page publishes per-tool carbon emissions. Users can see the environmental cost of each operation.

guidelines.title: Calculate the environmental impact
guidelines.detail: SCI profiler implements the Green Software Foundation specification to measure mgCO₂eq per tool invocation.

guidelines.title: Implement continuous improvement procedures
guidelines.detail: SCI history tracking compares carbon emissions across commits. Regressions are detectable over time.

guidelines.title: Establish if a digital product or service is necessary
guidelines.detail: PDF Worker replaces the need to upload files to remote servers for processing, reducing both privacy risk and network energy.

guidelines.title: Promote responsible data practices
guidelines.detail: Zero data collection by design. Files are processed in-browser and never transmitted. Privacy policy is explicit about this.

guidelines.title: Plan for a digital product or service's care and end-of-life
guidelines.detail: Static site with no server dependency. Even if the project is abandoned, the hosted version continues to work.

guidelines.title: Define performance and environmental budgets
guidelines.detail: SCI benchmarks define measurable carbon budgets per tool. Performance regressions are tracked in sci-history.json.

guidelines.title: Use open source where possible
guidelines.detail: All core dependencies are open source. The SCI profiler itself is published as an open-source library.
